safetyNote: This media map anchors approved visuals to content sections without changing the approved content map schema. Website-side image-field wiring remains a separate responsibility.
transferSafetyRule: Codefy OS never writes to Sanity. It only prepares media outputs.

entries.recommendedVisual: Attendance validation dashboard showing work location rules, user location checks, geofence validation, biometric confirmation, and employee transport eligibility in one enterprise control frame.
entries.recommendedPurpose: Support the page story around smart employee attendance, work-location validation, user-location validation, geofence validation, and biometric validation.
entries.sourceTruth: Faithful marketing recreation grounded in approved Tranzkit attendance validation, transport eligibility, geofence, and biometric workflows described in the approved Enterprises and Passengers content map.
entries.assetMethodNote: Use a Figma recreated mockup by default because the page needs a clear, polished attendance-governance visual and no verified safe product capture has been approved as launch-quality yet.

alt.en: Enterprise attendance validation dashboard showing work location, geofence, and biometric checks.
alt.ar: لوحة تحقق من حضور الموظفين تعرض التحقق من موقع العمل والنطاق الجغرافي والتحقق البيومتري.

entries.productionNotes: Wave 1 review asset generated in Figma as a shared-language-neutral marketing composite. It shows work location validation, user match, geofence checks, biometric confirmation, and transport eligibility inside one enterprise control surface.
entries.privacyNotes: This mockup uses safe demo group labels only. No personal names, phone numbers, emails, exact addresses, or raw employee identifiers are shown.
approvalChecklist: Keep attendance, work-location, geofence, and biometric proof readable at 1200x800.
Do not expose personal names, phone numbers, emails, exact addresses, or raw employee identifiers.
Keep AI cues embedded in the validation workflow and avoid autonomy claims.

entries.recommendedVisual: Executive and financial reporting view with attendance evidence, service validation, operator performance, finance review, and embedded AI reporting or controlled-update cards.
entries.recommendedPurpose: Support the page story around financial and executive reporting, operator performance insights, planned-versus-actual review, and controlled AI-assisted updates.
entries.sourceTruth: Faithful marketing recreation grounded in approved Tranzkit executive reporting, operator performance review, finance visibility, and controlled-update concepts already represented in the approved page story.
entries.assetMethodNote: Use a Figma recreated mockup by default because the page needs a high-signal enterprise reporting composition that stays safe, language-neutral, and visually stronger than a sparse raw finance capture.

alt.en: Enterprise reporting dashboard showing attendance, service, finance, and AI-assisted executive summaries.
alt.ar: لوحة تقارير مؤسسية تعرض الحضور والخدمة والمالية وملخصات تنفيذية مدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي.

approvalChecklist: Keep reporting, service, and finance proof points legible at 1200x800.
Do not expose sensitive finance values, bank details, private invoice references, or personal contact data.
Do not imply AI approves financial changes autonomously.

entries.recommendedVisual: Rider-facing visibility screen showing assigned trips, route timing, live service status, trip history context, and structured feedback readiness in a clean mobile-aware composition.
entries.recommendedPurpose: Support the page story around rider trip visibility, service confidence, clearer updates, and connected commute follow-up.
entries.sourceTruth: Faithful marketing recreation grounded in approved Tranzkit rider visibility, live status, trip history, and structured feedback concepts described in the approved Enterprises and Passengers content map.
entries.assetMethodNote: Use a Figma recreated mockup by default because the passenger-side story needs a clean visibility composition and no verified safe rider-facing product capture has been approved yet.

alt.en: Rider trip visibility screen showing assigned trips, live status, and service updates.
alt.ar: شاشة رؤية الراكب للرحلة تعرض الرحلات المخصصة والحالة المباشرة وتحديثات الخدمة.

approvalChecklist: Keep timing, status, and trip context readable without large embedded text.
Do not expose exact private addresses, phone numbers, emails, query strings, or raw trip identifiers.
Keep the passenger story connected to real Tranzkit rider visibility and feedback capabilities.

entries.recommendedVisual: Enterprise service validation view showing operator delivery, live service status, planned-versus-actual review, rider impact, and evidence-backed follow-up context.
entries.recommendedPurpose: Support the page story around service validation, operator performance, planned-versus-actual review, and enterprise accountability.
entries.sourceTruth: Faithful marketing recreation grounded in approved Tranzkit service validation, operator delivery review, rider visibility, and planned-versus-actual workflows described in the approved Enterprises and Passengers page.
entries.assetMethodNote: Use a Figma recreated mockup by default because the page needs a high-signal validation visual and no verified safe service-review capture has been approved as strong enough yet.

alt.en: Enterprise service validation view showing operator performance, live service status, and planned versus actual review.
alt.ar: شاشة تحقق مؤسسي من الخدمة تعرض أداء المشغلين وحالة الخدمة المباشرة ومراجعة المخطط بالفعلي.

entries.productionNotes: Wave 1 review asset generated in Figma as a shared-language-neutral marketing composite. It shows operator delivery, live status, planned-versus-actual review, rider impact, and evidence-backed follow-up in one enterprise review frame.
entries.privacyNotes: This mockup uses service-line labels and aggregate rider counts only. It does not expose personal rows, phone numbers, exact addresses, query strings, or raw trip identifiers.
approvalChecklist: Keep service validation, planned-versus-actual comparison, and operator accountability visible at first glance.
Do not expose phone numbers, emails, exact addresses, raw trip ids, query strings, or sensitive map details.
Stay faithful to supported Tranzkit delivery, review, and follow-up workflows.

mediaQualityRule.guidance: Tune app state before recreating in Figma. Recreated mockups must stay faithful to approved Tranzkit capabilities and approved scope.

mediaLanguagePolicy.currentRule: One image asset in Sanity, bilingual CMS text around it, and bilingual alt text in the media plan.
rules: Use a single shared media asset by default for English and Arabic pages.
Do not create separate English and Arabic images unless the asset contains large embedded text or truly needs RTL-specific layout treatment.
Avoid large English or Arabic text baked into images.
If a Figma frame needs text, keep it minimal and brand-neutral, such as the Tranzkit logo only.
If an asset ever becomes locale_specific_required, do not move it to ready_for_transfer until website-side localized image support or an approved workaround exists.
